mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market tightness eases sufficiently to trigger recession fears, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, or signal loses predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals robust aggregate demand, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (accuracy < 55%) or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing activity, which suppresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day returns in >65% of cases, or the signal shows no regime-dependent directional flip.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 2.0 percent, monetary policy becomes sufficiently restrictive to dampen risk appetite, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of observations where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0 percent, or threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic capacity and consumer spending power, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns (accuracy < 55%) or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising GDP growth and accelerating consumer price inflation creates stagflationary pressure, which compresses profit margins and forces negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL growth rates shows no predictive power for 20-day returns or forward returns are positive in >60% of high-inflation/high-growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production crosses below 105.0, manufacturing contraction signals imminent economic slowdown, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >65% of instances where INDPRO drops below 105.0, or the threshold signal loses predictive accuracy in walk-forward validation.
